trivia_bullets.text: Resurrected Cultist is a common black Human Cleric in Duskmourn: House of Horror (DSK, 2024-09-27), a horror plane consisting of a single supernatural haunted house. The Human Cleric typing places it within the cultist faction of the house's organized supernatural hierarchy.

Combines Delirium (four or more card types required in graveyard) with the Finality Counter. Delirium originated in Shadows over Innistrad (2016); its return in DSK is thematically coherent — both sets are horror planes with graveyard emphasis. The Finality Counter was introduced in The Lost Caverns of Ixalan (2023) to replace in-text 'exile instead of dying' reminder text with a visible trackable counter, resolving a decades-old memory issue.
sources: Draftsim: Finality Counters in MTG: Rules, History, and Best Cards

trivia_bullets.text: A 4/1 for {2}{B} — above-rate power balanced by 1 toughness. Self-recurses once via Delirium ({2}{B}{B}), but the attached Finality Counter prevents a second resurrection, capping the loop by design. EDHREC rank 23,837 — one of the lower-included DSK commons, reflecting the Delirium setup cost.
Illustrated by Tyler Walpole, a veteran MTG illustrator with credits across multiple Wizards sets.

related_cards.name: Rite of the Moth
related_cards.note: Another DSK black card that returns creatures with a Finality Counter — same mechanic pairing

related_cards.name: Gloomhunter

related_cards.name: Eternal Scourge
related_cards.note: Earlier design solution to the graveyard-escape-loop problem that the Finality Counter later resolved more elegantly
